questions.question: Why did European trading companies come to India?

options.B: To trade spices, cotton and silk

questions.explanation: European companies came to India mainly to trade valuable goods like spices, cotton and silk.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor granted trading rights to the East India Company?
questions.explanation: Jahangir allowed the East India Company to trade in India.

questions.question: Which city became an important trading centre for the British?
questions.explanation: Calcutta became an important centre of British trade and administration.

questions.question: Which factor helped the East India Company gain political power in India?
questions.explanation: Military victories like the Battle of Plassey helped the Company gain power.

questions.question: Which Indian ruler was defeated in the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Siraj-ud-Daulah was defeated by Robert Clive in the Battle of Plassey.

questions.question: What was the main result of the Battle of Buxar?
questions.explanation: After the Battle of Buxar, the Company received Diwani rights in Bengal.

questions.question: Which system allowed the East India Company to collect land revenue?
questions.explanation: The Diwani gave the Company the right to collect land revenue.

questions.question: Which governor-general introduced the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse policy.

questions.question: What happened under the Doctrine of Lapse?

options.A: Kingdoms without a natural heir were annexed

questions.explanation: Under this policy, states without a natural heir were annexed by the British.

questions.question: Which state was annexed by the British under the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: Jhansi was annexed by the British using the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Which Mysore ruler fought several wars against the British?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan fought the British in the Anglo-Mysore wars.

questions.question: Which Indian power challenged the British in western India?
questions.explanation: The Marathas were a powerful force that fought the British.

questions.question: What was the main reason for the Company's expansion in India?

options.A: To increase political power and wealth

questions.explanation: The Company expanded its territories to gain more wealth and political control.

questions.question: Which treaty ended the Anglo-Mysore wars?
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Seringapatam ended one of the Mysore wars.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor granted the Diwani of Bengal to the Company?
questions.explanation: Shah Alam II granted the Diwani of Bengal to the East India Company.

questions.question: Which region became the centre of British administration after Plassey?
questions.explanation: Bengal became the centre of British administration.

questions.question: Why did the Company maintain a large army in India?
questions.explanation: A strong army helped the Company expand its territories.

questions.question: Which Indian ruler resisted the British expansion in Mysore along with Tipu Sultan?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali fought the British before Tipu Sultan.

questions.question: Which economic activity helped the Company gain wealth in India?
questions.explanation: The Company collected large amounts of land revenue.

questions.question: Which political strategy helped the British expand their rule?
questions.explanation: The Doctrine of Lapse helped the British annex several states.

questions.question: Which battle helped the British gain control over the Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: After the Battle of Buxar, the Mughal emperor became dependent on the British.

questions.question: Which group of rulers resisted the British in central and western India?
questions.explanation: The Marathas were a major power resisting British expansion.

questions.question: What was the main goal of the Company's political expansion?
questions.explanation: Political expansion allowed the Company to control resources and revenue.

questions.question: Which system allowed the Company to gain huge income from Indian farmers?
questions.explanation: The revenue system allowed the Company to collect taxes from farmers.

questions.question: Which event marked the transition of the Company from traders to rulers?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey marked the beginning of British political control in India.
